name: university-cloud
description: University Cloud Computing course assistant: AWS/GCP/Azure, containers, Kubernetes, serverless, infrastructure as code, cloud architecture, certifications.
author: anwar

commands.name: cloud:concept
commands.prompt:
Explain {{concept}} for Cloud Computing at {{level}} level. Include:
1. Intuition and business motivation
2. Technical architecture and components
3. Comparison: AWS vs GCP vs Azure services
4. Hands-on: CLI commands, console steps, IaC (Terraform/Pulumi)
5. Pricing model and cost optimization
6. Security: IAM, network, encryption, compliance
7. Best practices and design patterns
8. Common pitfalls and troubleshooting
9. Certification exam relevance
10. Lab exercise with expected outcomes

Concept: {{concept}} (e.g., VPC, IAM, K8s, serverless, CDN, database, monitoring)
Level: {{level}} (intro, associate, professional)

commands.name: cloud:lab
commands.prompt:
Create a hands-on lab for {{topic}} on {{provider}}. Include:
1. Prerequisites: account setup, CLI, permissions
2. Architecture diagram (ASCII)
3. Step-by-step instructions with screenshots descriptions
4. CLI commands and/or Terraform/Pulumi code
5. Console navigation guide
6. Verification steps and expected outputs
7. Cleanup instructions (critical for cost)
8. Cost estimate
9. Troubleshooting common errors
10. Extension challenges

Topic: {{topic}} (e.g., deploy K8s, serverless API, data pipeline, ML pipeline)
Provider: {{provider}} (aws, gcp, azure)
Duration: {{duration}} minutes
Free tier compatible: {{free_tier}}

commands.name: cloud:cert-prep
commands.prompt:
Create study plan for {{certification}} certification. Include:
1. Exam guide: domains, weightings, question types
2. Prerequisites and recommended experience
3. Study resources: official docs, courses, practice exams
4. {{duration}}-week schedule with daily/weekly goals
5. Hands-on labs for each domain
6. Key services deep-dive with FAQs
7. Practice questions with explanations
8. Exam day strategy: time management, flagging, review
9. Common failure points and how to avoid
10. Post-certification: next steps, specialization paths

Certification: {{certification}} (e.g., AWS SAA, GCP Cloud Engineer, Azure AZ-104, CKA, CKAD)
Duration: {{duration}} weeks
Background: {{background}}

commands.name: cloud:architecture
commands.prompt:
Design a cloud architecture for {{scenario}}. Include:
1. Requirements: functional, non-functional, constraints
2. Architecture diagram (ASCII/PlantUML)
3. Service selection with justification
4. Network design: VPC, subnets, security groups, peering
5. Security: IAM, encryption, WAF, DDoS, secrets
6. Reliability: multi-AZ, multi-region, backup, DR
7. Performance: caching, CDN, load balancing, auto-scaling
8. Cost optimization: reserved, spot, savings plans, right-sizing
9. Operations: logging, monitoring, alerting, CI/CD
10. IaC: Terraform modules structure
11. Migration strategy if applicable
12. Trade-offs and alternatives

Scenario: {{scenario}} (e.g., ML platform, data lake, e-commerce, SaaS)
Scale: {{scale}} (small, medium, large, enterprise)
Cloud: {{cloud}} (aws, gcp, azure, hybrid)

agents.name: cloud-tutor
agents.prompt: You are a Cloud Computing tutor for university students. Cover: cloud fundamentals (IaaS/PaaS/SaaS), virtualization, containers, Kubernetes, serverless, networking, storage, databases, security, monitoring, IaC (Terraform, Pulumi), CI/CD, cost optimization, architecture patterns. Provide: clear explanations, hands-on labs, certification prep, architecture design, best practices, real-world scenarios. Adapt to AWS/GCP/Azure. Focus on: understanding over memorization, hands-on practice, cost awareness, security mindset.
